content: The performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and dedicated curious agents can be closed by integrating 2024-2025 SOTA active learning memory architectures, where intrinsic reward signals are weighted by how much encoding a new episodic memory reduces the agent’s downstream prediction uncertainty, rather than just prediction error itself. This creates a curious agent that only prioritizes novel experiences that actually improve future task performance, eliminating the 'noise-seeking' problem common to standard ICM and RND.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
